channel.title: Libertas - Freedom Tech Signals
channel.description: Curated insights on freedom technology: Bitcoin, privacy tools, censorship resistance, and digital sovereignty.

item.title: Bukele Admin Escalates Financial Censorship Against El Faro, Freezes Personal Assets
item.description: El Salvador's government froze personal bank accounts and property of El Faro shareholders, escalating financial warfare against independent media that previously faced Pegasus surveillance.

item.title: Tunisia Prosecutes Refugee Aid Workers Under Travel Document Laws
item.description: Tunisia sentenced two refugee aid workers to 2 years prison for providing accommodation to asylum seekers, part of broader crackdown shutting down civil society groups.

item.title: Proton Mail Adds Post-Quantum Encryption to Protect Against Future Threats
item.description: Proton Mail launches optional post-quantum encryption for all users to protect encrypted emails against future quantum computer attacks that could break current cryptography.

item.title: Access Now Report: Data Privacy as Defense Against Electoral Disinformation in Latin America
item.description: Access Now analysis shows personal data collection fuels political microtargeting and disinformation, recommending strict data protection enforcement during elections across Latin America.

item.title: Indonesia Plans Government Vetting System for Human Rights Defenders
item.description: Indonesia's government announced plans to create an assessment team to determine who qualifies as a 'genuine' human rights defender, following acid attack on activist Andrie Yunus by soldiers.

item.title: Myanmar Journalists Use Encrypted Networks to Bypass Junta Censorship
item.description: Myanmar journalists in exile and inside the country use encrypted communications and underground networks to continue reporting despite systematic media crackdowns, forming a distributed newsroom across borders.

item.title: RightsCon 2026 Canceled After Chinese Government Pressure on Zambia
item.description: The world's largest digital rights conference was abruptly canceled in Zambia after Chinese government pressure to exclude Taiwanese participants and moderate discussions, reflecting growing transnational repression of civil society.

item.title: DOJ Criminalizes FOIA Evasion in First-Ever Case Against Retired NIH Scientist
item.description: Trump DOJ charged retired NIH scientist David Morens with felony crimes for using personal email to evade FOIA requests, marking unprecedented criminalization of transparency law violations.

item.title: EFF Releases Guide to Combat Digital Surveillance Abuses in the Americas
item.description: EFF published comprehensive guide outlining legal frameworks and institutional safeguards needed to curb systematic digital surveillance abuses across Latin America, leveraging Inter-American human rights law.

item.title: Tunisia Expands Press Crackdown Beyond Criminal Charges to Financial Warfare
item.description: Tunisia's government is systematically dismantling independent media through financial crime charges, banking freezes, and dissolution proceedings against outlets like Inkyfada, beyond traditional prosecutions.

item.title: Citizen Lab Director Warns OSCE About Unregulated Surveillance Marketplace
item.description: Ron Deibert told OSCE that mercenary spyware is just the tip of a massive, unregulated surveillance economy targeting civil society.

item.title: EFF Launches Anti-Surveillance Campaign, Releases Rayhunter IMSI Catcher Detection Tool
item.description: EFF launches 'Claw Back' campaign against mass surveillance as Congress considers Section 702 renewal, highlighting new open-source Rayhunter tool for detecting cell-site simulators used to track activists and journalists.

item.title: Why 'Anonymized' Data Isn't Really Anonymous: 15 Data Points ID 99.98% of People
item.description: Proton explains how anonymized data can still identify individuals with just 15 data points achieving 99.98% identification rates, and how AI is making re-identification faster and cheaper.

item.title: China's New Xinjiang Secrecy Law Embeds AI Surveillance Into Social Governance
item.description: China's new state secrets regulation for Xinjiang uses AI monitoring and grassroots reporting to control information flows, making everyday communication subject to security evaluation and creating a 'structure of silence.'

item.title: ICE Protesters Sue DHS/FBI Over Forced DNA Collection During Arrests
item.description: Four protesters filed suit against DHS and FBI for allegedly collecting DNA samples from Americans arrested during peaceful ICE protests in Chicago, claiming permanent storage in federal databases violates constitutional rights.

item.title: Microsoft-G42 Data Center in Kenya Raises Digital Sovereignty Concerns
item.description: Microsoft and G42's $1B data center project in Kenya mirrors historical exploitation patterns, raising concerns about foreign control of national infrastructure, tax avoidance, and digital colonialism.

item.title: El Salvador Detains Anti-Corruption Lawyer for One Year, Triggers Mass Exodus of Rights Groups
item.description: Salvadoran authorities have held prominent anti-corruption lawyer Ruth López in sealed pretrial detention for one year, sparking wider crackdown that forced 140+ activists and journalists into exile.

item.title: Rights Groups Demand Microsoft Disclose Review of Israeli Military AI/Cloud Services
item.description: Five major rights organizations are pressuring Microsoft to publish findings of its review into Israeli military use of Azure and AI services for surveillance and targeting of Palestinians.

item.title: EFF Challenges Weak Science Behind State Social Media Bans for Youth
item.description: EFF argues that state legislatures are rushing to ban youth social media access based on flawed research and 'pop psychology' rather than settled science, threatening constitutional rights.

item.title: EFF Campaigns for Saudi Wikipedian Imprisoned 14 Years for Human Rights Edits
item.description: Saudi authorities imprisoned Wikipedia contributor Osama Khalid for 14 years after he documented human rights abuses and criticized government surveillance on encrypted platforms.

item.title: Portland AI Drone Targeting Firm Ships Hardware to Israeli Military Contractor
item.description: Sightline Intelligence, a Portland-based AI targeting company, made 10+ shipments of surveillance hardware to Israeli weapons giant Elbit Systems, sparking local activist protests.

item.title: EFF Coalition Opposes UK Age-Gating Requirements That Threaten Web Anonymity
item.description: EFF and 18 organizations warn UK policymakers that proposed age verification requirements would undermine internet privacy and anonymity while fragmenting the open web into restricted jurisdictions.

item.title: Trump Admin Targets 'Anti-Fascists' as Terrorists in 2026 Counterterrorism Strategy
item.description: Trump's new counterterrorism strategy formally classifies 'Violent Left-Wing Extremists, including Anti-Fascists' as major terror threats alongside ISIS and cartels, while omitting right-wing groups.

item.title: Law Firm Seeks to Redistribute $344M Frozen USDT from Iran-Related Seizure
item.description: US law firm Gerstein Harrow is attempting to claim $344M in frozen USDT for clients with unrelated judgments, demonstrating how seized crypto assets become targets for redistribution.

item.title: EFF Publishes Comprehensive Guide to Circumventing Internet Shutdowns
item.description: EFF released a practical guide covering mesh networks, peer-to-peer messaging, ham radio, and satellite internet for maintaining communications during government shutdowns and disasters.

item.title: EFF Warns Federal SECURE Data Act Would Weaken Existing State Privacy Protections
item.description: EFF says the Republican-backed SECURE Data Act would preempt dozens of state privacy laws while offering weaker consumer protections and no private enforcement rights.

item.title: EFF Challenges Warrantless Border Device Searches in Fourth Circuit
item.description: EFF filed amicus brief in U.S. v. Belmonte Cardozo urging Fourth Circuit to require warrants for all electronic device searches at borders, as CBP conducted 55,318 warrantless device searches in FY2025.

item.title: Rwandan YouTuber Dies in Prison Hours Before Release After 5-Year Sentence
item.description: Aimable Karasira Uzaramba, a Rwandan YouTube commentator who criticized the government, died of alleged overdose in state custody on his scheduled release day after 5 years imprisonment.

item.title: Maldives Government Raids Independent News Outlet, Seizes Digital Equipment
item.description: Maldives police raided Adhadhu News office, seizing laptops and hard drives over documentary alleging presidential misconduct, while imposing travel bans on journalists.

item.title: OONI Report: Internet Censorship Becoming More Sophisticated and Targeted
item.description: OONI's network measurement data reveals internet censorship is evolving toward more sophisticated, targeted, and intermittent blocks that are harder to detect globally.

item.title: US Congress Threatens to Criminalize Crypto Developers, Undermine Freedom Tech
item.description: Critical developer protections in the BRCA could be stripped from market structure legislation, leaving non-custodial software creators facing criminal prosecution like Tornado Cash and Samourai Wallet developers.

item.title: DOJ Pursues Rare Federal Felony Charges Against Journalists Covering Minnesota Protest
item.description: Committee to Protect Journalists and 16 civil society groups urge DOJ to drop federal charges, including felony conspiracy, against four journalists arrested while covering immigration enforcement protest.

item.title: FBI Closed Investigation Into Student Activist While ICE Continued Detention
item.description: FBI documents reveal the agency closed investigation into Palestinian activist Mahmoud Khalil after determining he posed no threat, yet ICE continued detention based on anonymous tip claiming Hamas support.

item.title: Burkina Faso Junta Bans TV5Monde, Escalates Information Control Campaign
item.description: Burkina Faso's military government suspended French broadcaster TV5Monde for 'disinformation,' continuing systematic media crackdown since 2022 coup including journalist disappearances and coordinated online propaganda.

item.title: Access Now Hosts Digital Security Webinar Series for Civil Society in Conflict Zones
item.description: Access Now's Digital Security Helpline is running a webinar series focusing on protecting civil society and human rights defenders from digital threats during armed conflicts and humanitarian crises.

item.title: Canada's Bill C-22 Mandates Surveillance Backdoors, Threatens Encryption
item.description: Canada's Bill C-22 would force tech companies to build surveillance backdoors, retain metadata for a year, and could ban strong encryption despite vague 'systemic vulnerability' exceptions.

item.title: Major Bitcoin Mining Pools Join Stratum v2 Working Group for Mining Decentralization
item.description: Seven major Bitcoin mining entities including ANTPOOL, Block Inc, and F2Pool joined the Stratum v2 Working Group to advance adoption of the protocol that improves mining decentralization and security.

item.title: Human Rights Watch: EU Surveillance Tech Exports Enable Global Rights Violations
item.description: Human Rights Watch report reveals EU's 2021 Dual-Use Regulation is failing to prevent surveillance technology exports to governments that use them to spy on activists and journalists.

item.title: DHS Testing Autonomous Surveillance Drones on US-Canada Border
item.description: DHS will deploy autonomous drones and ground vehicles streaming battlefield intelligence over 5G networks along the US-Canada border in a bilateral experiment this fall.

item.title: Venezuelan Journalist Still Faces Charges Despite Post-Maduro Amnesty Law
item.description: Venezuelan journalist Rory Branker was released after Maduro's ouster but denied amnesty, highlighting inconsistent application of freedom protections and ongoing press censorship via website blocking requiring VPNs.

item.title: Utah Becomes First State to Regulate VPN Use for Age Verification Bypass
item.description: Utah's new law, effective May 6, 2026, targets VPN usage to circumvent age verification, requiring companies to verify ages based on physical location regardless of VPN use and banning instruction sharing.

item.title: GUARD Act Would End Anonymous Internet Use via Mandatory ID Checks for AI Tools
item.description: The GUARD Act, framed as AI safety for minors, would require government ID verification for all users of AI-powered tools including search engines, effectively ending anonymous internet use.

item.title: US House Whip Pushes Anti-CBDC Act to Block Government Digital Currency
item.description: House Majority Whip Tom Emmer is advocating for his Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act, which passed the House but awaits Senate action to permanently ban central bank digital currencies.

item.title: Proton Mail Rolls Out Post-Quantum Encryption to All Users
item.description: Proton Mail now offers post-quantum cryptography protection against future quantum computer attacks, available free to all users with OpenPGP v6 support.

item.title: EFF Warns of Government Censorship Disguised as Online Safety Measures
item.description: EFF's latest analysis reveals how governments are using 'child safety' as pretext to implement dangerous censorship systems, including social media bans and VPN restrictions.

item.title: Iran Launches Bitcoin-Settled Maritime Insurance for Strait of Hormuz Transit
item.description: Iran launched Hormuz Safe, a Bitcoin-settled maritime insurance platform for Strait of Hormuz shipping, projecting $10B revenue while leveraging Bitcoin's censorship resistance under US sanctions.

item.title: Trump Admin Counterterrorism Strategy Targets Anti-Fascist Activists, Domestic Dissent
item.description: New 16-page Trump counterterrorism strategy explicitly targets anti-fascist activists alongside ISIS, with 'find you and kill you' language, expanding surveillance powers against domestic dissent.

item.title: ICE Developing Smart Glasses with Facial Recognition Database Integration
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement is developing smart glasses equipped with facial recognition capabilities tied to government databases, expanding pervasive surveillance infrastructure.

item.title: Rights Groups Demand Microsoft Disclose Review of Azure Services to Israeli Military Surveillance
item.description: Access Now and coalition demand Microsoft publicly release findings of investigation into Azure cloud services used by Israeli military units for mass surveillance and AI-assisted targeting of Palestinians.

item.title: Samourai Wallet Developer Seeks Presidential Pardon Amid Trump Clemency Considerations
item.description: Developer behind privacy-focused Samourai Wallet crypto mixer seeks presidential pardon as Trump weighs 250 clemency decisions, highlighting legal pressure on Bitcoin privacy tools.

item.title: EFF Urges EU to Prioritize Privacy and User Sovereignty in Digital Fairness Act
item.description: EFF calls for EU's proposed Digital Fairness Act to ban dark patterns, tackle surveillance-based business models, and strengthen user control rather than expanding platform surveillance powers.

item.title: DR Congo Escalates Repression of Journalists, Activists Amid Constitutional Crisis
item.description: Congo authorities increasingly detaining journalists, activists, and opposition members amid constitutional amendment tensions and M23 conflict, with intelligence agencies conducting enforced disappearances and arbitrary arrests.

item.title: Meta Removes Instagram's End-to-End Encryption Feature, Cites Low Adoption
item.description: Instagram ended its opt-in end-to-end encryption feature, breaking Meta's promise to encrypt DMs by default and eliminating privacy protections on a major platform.

item.title: EFF Highlights How Commercial Tracking Feeds Government Mass Surveillance
item.description: EFF details how widespread commercial tracking enables government surveillance, with data brokers selling personal info to FBI/CBP/ICE to bypass warrant requirements.

item.title: Signal Threatens to Exit Canada Over Encryption-Breaking Bill C-22
item.description: Signal's VP of Strategy says the encrypted messaging app would rather leave Canada than comply with Bill C-22, which could force backdoors into end-to-end encryption.

item.title: White House to Announce Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Details After Legal Framework Completion
item.description: U.S. officials say announcement on Strategic Bitcoin Reserve implementation is imminent after clearing legal hurdles for custody of 328,372 BTC held from law enforcement seizures.

item.title: Press Freedom Orgs Document Federal Targeting of Independent Journalists Under Trump
item.description: CPJ and Amnesty International highlight arrests of multiple independent journalists covering immigration enforcement protests, warning of systematic targeting by federal government.

item.title: Tails 7.7.3 Emergency Release Fixes Critical Kernel Vulnerability
item.description: Tails released emergency update fixing 'Dirty Frag' kernel vulnerability that could allow privilege escalation and user deanonymization, plus Tor Browser and client security patches.

item.title: Tunisia Prosecutes Refugee Aid Workers, Shuts Down Civil Society Organizations
item.description: Tunisia sentenced two refugee aid workers to prison for humanitarian work, shut down their organization, and froze accounts as part of broader crackdown on civil society groups.

item.title: EFF: Revised GUARD Act Still Threatens Privacy Despite Narrowed Scope
item.description: Congress narrowed the GUARD Act to focus on AI companions rather than all AI systems, but EFF warns mandatory age verification still creates serious privacy, speech, and access concerns.

item.title: Major Financial Firms Block Donations to SPLC After DOJ Indictment
item.description: Fidelity, Vanguard, and Schwab are blocking donor-advised fund donations to SPLC following Trump DOJ indictment, demonstrating financial censorship without due process.

item.title: Senate Confirms Bitcoin-Friendly Kevin Warsh to Fed Board
item.description: Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh to Fed Board 51-45, clearing path to replace Jerome Powell as Fed Chair. Warsh calls bitcoin 'important asset' and 'good policeman for policy.'

item.title: US Senate Banking Committee to Vote on Crypto Clarity Act Despite Opposition
item.description: Senate Banking Committee will vote Thursday on crypto regulation bill facing pushback from banks and Democrats, potentially impacting financial sovereignty landscape.

item.title: CPJ Urges Trump to Secure Jimmy Lai's Release in Xi Meeting
item.description: Committee to Protect Journalists calls on Trump to prioritize jailed Hong Kong publisher Jimmy Lai's release during diplomatic talks with Xi Jinping, highlighting broader press freedom concerns.

item.title: DOJ Launches Criminal Investigation Into Trans Youth Healthcare Providers
item.description: Trump DOJ issued criminal grand jury subpoenas to hospitals including NYU Langone demanding records of trans youth patients and medical staff, escalating from previous civil investigations.

item.title: Apple & Google Deploy E2E Encrypted Cross-Platform Messaging via RCS
item.description: Apple's iOS 26.5 enables end-to-end encryption for RCS messages between iPhone and Android, making encrypted cross-platform messaging the default for billions of users.

item.title: Meta Enables Coordinated Attacks on Bangladeshi Protest Archivists Through False Copyright Claims
item.description: Coordinated attackers are exploiting Meta's copyright system with fake claims to remove Facebook pages documenting human rights violations from Bangladesh's July 2024 uprising, destroying evidence used in war crimes tribunals.

item.title: Uzbek Activist Lawyer Gets 18+ Years for Protests, Prison 'Disruption'
item.description: Karakalpak lawyer Dauletmurat Tazhimuratov received additional 5-year sentence for 'disrupting prison work,' bringing total to 18.5 years for his role in 2022 protests.

item.title: Tunisia Escalates Media Crackdown Beyond Individual Prosecutions to Target Infrastructure
item.description: Tunisia is dismantling independent media infrastructure through banking freezes, dissolution proceedings, and financial crime charges while continuing to jail journalists under Decree-Law 54.

item.title: California Fast-Tracks Social Media Ban Requiring ID Verification for All Users
item.description: California's A.B. 1709 would ban under-16s from social media and force all users to submit government IDs or biometrics for age verification, destroying online anonymity.

item.title: Signal Threatens Canada Exit Over Lawful Access Bill C-22
item.description: Signal and Meta oppose Canada's Bill C-22, which could mandate metadata collection for messaging apps. Signal threatens to pull out of Canada if forced to comply with the surveillance requirements.

item.title: Congress Narrows GUARD Act, But Privacy-Invasive Age Verification Requirements Remain
item.description: Congress revised the GUARD Act to target AI companions rather than all AI systems, but still mandates identity-linked age verification and increases penalties to $250K per violation.
